## RADAR chart, SAGA style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-MAG-07",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "magnitude",
    "comparison"
  ],
  "form": "3-6 shared-unit axes from a center; one polygon per series",
  "data_required": "3-6 dimensions ON THE SAME SCALE for at most 2 series",
  "family": "MAG",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-MAG-02 (paired bars read better)",
    "CH-RNK-01 (single series)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "axes have different units",
      "use": "CH-MAG-02"
    },
    {
      "when": "one series only",
      "use": "CH-RNK-01"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- WARN: only when every axis shares one unit and the audience expects a radar
- profile shapes across 3-6 same-unit dimensions, 2 series max

Do NOT use when:
- different units per axis (the polygon area means nothing)
- more than 2 series (unreadable overlap)
- any case a paired bar answers (most cases)

### Input data shape

```json
{
  "title": "action title with a number",
  "source": "bottom-left always",
  "unit": "shared by every axis, printed at the top ring",
  "axes": "3-6 dimension names",
  "series": "1-2 {name, values}"
}
```

### Rules
- every axis one unit, one shared 0-based scale (validated intent)
- polygon area is NOT a measure; the shape is the only message
- 2 series maximum, first is the key

### Never do this
- WARN ENTRY: radars are usually the wrong answer; paired bars read faster and more honestly
- mixed units across axes
- 3+ series
- reading enclosed area as a score

### QA before delivering
- [ ] axes share one unit
- [ ] <= 2 series
- [ ] zero-based rings
- [ ] source present

| background | `#FFFFFF` |
| gridline | `#E4E4E4` |

| tick_label | `#2E2E2E` |
| series_a | `#C0182B` |
| series_b | `#B0B0B0` |
| title | `#111111` |
| source | `#2E2E2E` |

- Title font: Georgia,serif, weight 700
- All other text: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 400 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

No kicker, no eyebrow; title at the default y.
